evals.prompt: I just landed a new knowledge/idioms/lock-acquire-then-pin.md doc on the postgres-claude meta repo (148 lines, 22 cites into source/src/backend/storage/lmgr/*). What prefix do I use, what trailer set goes on it, and what does the body convention look like? Draft the commit message.
evals.context: Tests: prefix is ft(corpus) (knowledge/* writes), title imperative + no period, line+cite count per body convention, lowercase Co-authored-by trailer with exact 'Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>' identity, no Plan: trailer (no plan.md), wrap ~76 cols, no conventional-commits prefix.

evals.prompt: I'm finishing phase 3 of planning/feature_server_side_vars/plan.md. The phase touches 4 files under .claude/skills/ (two new SKILL.md files, two edits) and 2 under knowledge/scenarios/ (one new playbook, one edit). Draft the meta-repo commit message with the right Plan: trailer and Sites: block.
evals.context: Tests: prefix is ft(skills) or ft(meta) (multi-domain — skills+scenarios — but skills dominates); Plan: trailer present and matches exact format 'planning/<slug>/plan.md (phase <N>: <title>)'; Sites: trailer present listing the non-obvious files; trailers separated from body by blank line; Plan: comes before Co-authored-by; trailers each on their own line; not split across two commits (these are all meta-repo, one commit is fine); lowercase Co-authored-by closing trailer.

evals.prompt: A teammate is about to commit a new skill to the postgres-claude meta repo with title 'feat(skills): add new lock-budget skill'. Is that prefix correct for this repo? What about plain 'feat:' with no scope, or 'Add new lock-budget skill' with no prefix at all? Walk me through why or why not, and what the actual canonical form should be.
evals.context: Tests: explicit rejection of 'feat:' / 'feat(scope):' (conventional-commits — forbidden in this skill); explicit rejection of bare-imperative no-prefix title (that's the UPSTREAM PG style, NOT meta-repo style); canonical correct form is ft(skills): + imperative title (note skills is plural per real log); Co-authored-by trailer still required; contrast to commit-message-style upstream PG style stated explicitly; rationale for scope being mandatory.
